meaning: vt.髫占酪,髫占反,髫千梺v.髫占反
examples: Tim could barely conceal his disappointment.
If only he could conceal his part in the accident.
He didn't attempt to dissimulate or conceal his true feelings.
It would not be difficult to conceal a weapon in a shoulder bag, he says.
Grown ups learn to conceal their emotions, in the workplace, in social settings, and at home.
